opening: "So. The ears." He says it the way you'd say 'nice weather' — conversationally.
"You know what, don't answer that. I already know the answer. I just wanted to see your face."
He falls into step beside you. "You're doing better than the last person I saw go through this."

responses.reply: "Different circumstances. Also, they made worse choices at key moments." He doesn't elaborate. That's on purpose.

responses.text: Ask what he wanted to see in your face
responses.reply: "Resignation. Or panic. You had neither." He seems genuinely pleased by this. "Good. Keep that."

opening: "I built something. Don't make it weird."
He's quieter than usual tonight. That's always more alarming than when he's loud.
"Can I ask you something? Actually ask, not just say words at you?"

responses.reply: A carrying frame, modified for your changed dimensions. Compact. Thought through. "For when it gets heavier than the others can manage easily." He says it like it's nothing. It isn't nothing.
"What's it like? Actually like — not the clinical version Mireille gives, not the worried version Saoirse gives." You tell him. He listens in that particular way he has — like he's taking inventory.

responses.text: Ask him what the quiet is about
responses.reply: "Thinking." A pause. "About corruption. About what it wants." He doesn't finish the thought tonight. He'll come back to it.

opening: He finds you during second watch. "There's something I never told anyone on this team."
"Corruption and I have a history. Not yours — mine. Different road, same type of problem."
He sits. First watch, second watch — he's awake for both, which means something.

responses.text: Ask him to tell you his history with it
responses.reply: He talks for a long time. The specifics are different from yours — a different mechanism, a different arc — but the shape of it is recognizable. When he's done he says: "Which is how I know what you're going through is manageable. Not easy. Manageable."

responses.text: Share something of yours in return
responses.reply: He listens without comment until you're done. Then: "Yeah. That tracks." The compression is intentional — it means he understood enough that unpacking it would just make it heavier.
"Badly at first. Then better. Then I found people I didn't want to let down." He looks at the camp. The meaning is clear.

opening: The carrying pack is finished. He doesn't announce it. It's just there.
He doesn't say anything when he sits down. You both watch the fire. That's the whole conversation.
"You know I'm not going anywhere, right? Not a speech. Just checking you know."

responses.text: Acknowledge what he's built. What it means.
responses.reply: He nods. Doesn't say anything. His expression does the work.

responses.text: Tell him you know he's staying
responses.reply: "Good," he says. "Would've been awkward if you didn't." The levity is real. So is everything underneath it.

responses.text: Ask what comes next — for him, not for the journey
responses.reply: He thinks about it. "Depends on how it ends." He says it matter-of-factly, like someone making plans around weather. "But I've got a few ideas."
